summary: Fire‑filled Weather Report Suite, epic Spanish Jam & soaring Garcia
blurb: The second set erupts with the Weather Report Suite rolling into a legendary Spanish Jam that segues flawlessly into Eyes Of The World and China Doll. Jerry’s guitar is in peak form, Phil drives the band with beast‑like bass, and Keith adds jazzy piano sparkle. The first set builds strong momentum with tight renditions of Bertha, Deal and Scarlet Begonias.
review: July 19, 1974 at Selland Arena captures a Grateful Dead at one of their creative peaks. From the opening “Bertha” through a punchy “Deal”, the first set lays down solid ground before launching into a monster second half. The Weather Report Suite Prelude and Part 1 blossom into “Let It Grow,” which then bursts into the soaring Spanish Jam, flowing straight into an inspired “Eyes Of The World” and a heartfelt “China Doll.” Reviewers across five soundboard sources describe Jerry’s tone as “perfect,” “un‑real,” and his solos as the best of ’74, while Phil is hailed as a “beast” in the “Phil Zone,” driving the jams with thunderous bass. Keith’s piano shines on the Spanish Jam and Let It Grow, adding jazzy color, and Bill Kreutzmann’s snappy snare provides a tight rhythmic foundation. The show’s energy is captured brilliantly on the recommended Gans‑Finney SBD: crisp, well‑balanced mix that lets every nuance shine. Even the solid matrix capture holds up with warm wall‑of‑sound ambience, while audience tapes, though varied in fidelity, still convey the night’s high‑octane vibe. In short, this performance is a must‑listen for anyone craving that mid‑’70s Dead fire.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six soundboard analyses are overwhelmingly positive, repeatedly praising the Weather Report Suite > Let It Grow > Spanish Jam > Eyes sequence, Jerry’s peak guitar tone, Phil’s beast‑like bass and Keith’s jazzy piano. No consistent negative comments about playing were found; only isolated remarks on mix level for a few songs. This uniform acclaim yields a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crystal‑clear mix, perfect instrument separation and no dropouts; captures the full energy of the performance.

key_highlights: Monstrous Weather Report Suite > Let It Grow > Spanish Jam > Eyes Of The World sequence
"Scarlet Begonias" into "Playing In The Band" showcase tight first‑set momentum
Jerry Garcia’s peak guitar tone and soaring solos throughout the night
Keith Godchaux’s jazzy piano adds sparkle to the Spanish Jam and Let It Grow

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers his best‑of‑’74 playing – tone described as perfect and un‑real, soaring solos on the Weather Report Suite, Spanish Jam and China Doll that many call peak Garcia.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh is a beast in the "Phil Zone," laying down dominant, bomb‑dropping bass lines that drive the Spanish Jam and Let It Grow, with standout solo moments in Eyes Of The World.
band_performance.Bob: Weir provides solid rhythm work; early mix issues noted but overall his guitar adds groove and he’s loose and lively during the Spanish → Eyes transition.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s snappy snare and tight, energetic drumming underpin the jams, delivering a reliable backbone throughout both sets.
